lakeb2b_master_reference:1.heading: MASTER REFERENCE DOCUMENT
lakeb2b_master_reference:1.text:
Product  |  Pricing  |  Sales  |  Operations  |  Brand  |  FAQ
Version 1.0  |  April 2026

lakeb2b_master_reference:2.heading: 1.1 Who We Are
lakeb2b_master_reference:2.text:
LakeB2B is a B2B data intelligence company that sits within the Champions Group portfolio under the Data & Services ecosystem. The name "LakeB2B" is intentional: the "Lake" represents depth, a deep reservoir of verified, actionable business data, and "B2B" signals our core commitment to business-to-business partnerships and client outcomes.
We are not a list vendor. We are a data intelligence partner. The distinction matters because LakeB2B invests in the outcome (meetings booked, pipeline built, campaigns that convert) rather than simply delivering rows in a spreadsheet.

lakeb2b_master_reference:3.heading: 1.3 Mission
lakeb2b_master_reference:3.text: LakeB2B exists to help businesses grow faster through data-driven intelligence. Every product, every solution vertical, and every client engagement is rooted in one belief: that accurate, compliant, intent-driven data is the engine behind every high-performing revenue team.

lakeb2b_master_reference:4.heading: 1.4 Where LakeB2B Sits in the Champions Group Portfolio
lakeb2b_master_reference:4.text: Champions Group is a multi-venture innovation group headquartered in Bangalore, overseeing 12 companies across four ecosystems. LakeB2B sits in the Data & Services ecosystem alongside SPAN Global Services, IP Momentum, and Cirralogix.

lakeb2b_master_reference:5.heading: 1.5 What Makes LakeB2B Different
lakeb2b_master_reference:5.text:
In conversations with prospects, clients, and partners, these five differentiators should anchor everything you say:
Data quality over data volume. Bounce rates consistently under 1% versus the industry average of 5 to 8%. We lead with deliverability, not record count.
Intent-enriched records. Every contact comes layered with behavioural signals, technographic data, and account-level intent so your outreach is targeted, not random.
Compliance from day one. GDPR, CCPA, DPDP: we do not treat compliance as an afterthought. Our infrastructure is built around privacy frameworks from the ground up.
Vertical specialization. Four distinct Tech verticals (SalesTech, MarTech, RecruitTech, GrowthTech) mean the buyer gets a solution tuned for their specific team, not a generic data dump.

lakeb2b_master_reference:6.heading: 1.6 Key Metrics to Know
lakeb2b_master_reference:6.text: Memorize these. They come up in every client conversation, pitch, and internal review.

lakeb2b_master_reference:7.heading: 2. Product Overview: The Four Verticals
lakeb2b_master_reference:7.text: LakeB2B organizes its solutions across four verticals, each named with a "Tech" suffix to signal technology-backed intelligence. All verticals share the same underlying data infrastructure but are tuned for different buyer teams and use cases.

lakeb2b_master_reference:8.heading: What SalesTech Solves
lakeb2b_master_reference:8.text:
SDR and BDR teams burning time on outdated, unverified contact data
High email bounce rates that damage domain reputation and sender score
Low reply rates caused by targeting the wrong person at the wrong time
Outbound teams struggling to scale into new geographies or verticals without reliable local data
Pipeline velocity bottlenecks caused by manual list building and research

lakeb2b_master_reference:9.heading: Key Results to Quote
lakeb2b_master_reference:9.text:
Bounce rate reduction to under 1% (from industry-typical 5 to 8%)
3x to 5x reply rate lift from intent-enriched targeting
45% faster pipeline build compared to manual prospecting
Domain reputation protection through pre-verified email deliverability

lakeb2b_master_reference:10.heading: SalesTech Use Cases
lakeb2b_master_reference:10.text:
New Territory Expansion: SDR team entering APAC needs compliant, region-specific contacts with direct dials and verified emails.
CRM Refresh: Existing CRM has decayed 30%+ over 18 months; SalesTech re-verifies, enriches, and fills gaps.
ABM List Build: Sales team running an account-based play on 200 target accounts; needs multi-threaded contacts per account.
Event Follow-Up Enrichment: Post-conference lead list is incomplete; SalesTech fills in titles, direct dials, and intent scores.

lakeb2b_master_reference:11.heading: What MarTech Solves
lakeb2b_master_reference:11.text:
Marketing teams targeting too broadly, wasting budget on unqualified impressions
High cost-per-MQL driven by poor audience precision
Low campaign relevance and engagement from stale or generic databases
Database hygiene problems that clog nurture flows and distort reporting
Inability to build compliant, verified audiences for programmatic and ABM programs

lakeb2b_master_reference:12.heading: Key Results to Quote
lakeb2b_master_reference:12.text:
200%+ campaign ROI improvement across MarTech client base
30% reduction in customer acquisition cost (CAC)
5x lead acquisition lift from precision audience targeting
Significant improvement in email deliverability and sender reputation for campaign lists

lakeb2b_master_reference:13.heading: What RecruitTech Solves
lakeb2b_master_reference:13.text:
Talent acquisition teams struggling with sourcing speed in niche or hard-to-fill skill sets
Inability to reach passive candidates who are not active on job boards
Scaling hiring across new markets without local talent data infrastructure
Over-reliance on LinkedIn InMail with declining response rates
Need for verified direct contact data (personal email, mobile) for executive and specialist roles

lakeb2b_master_reference:14.heading: Key Results to Quote
lakeb2b_master_reference:14.text:
40% faster time-to-shortlist compared to traditional sourcing
Access to 450M+ global executive profiles, including passive candidates
Verified direct dials and personal emails for hard-to-reach talent
Niche skill-set sourcing that traditional job boards cannot match

lakeb2b_master_reference:15.heading: What GrowthTech Solves
lakeb2b_master_reference:15.text:
CXOs and RevOps leaders who need a bespoke data strategy, not off-the-shelf lists
Compliance requirements across multiple jurisdictions (GDPR, DPDP, CCPA) that generic providers cannot satisfy
Custom ICP modelling and territory planning that requires deep data architecture
Organizations outgrowing their current data vendor and needing an infrastructure partner
Board-level reporting that demands auditable, defensible data sourcing

lakeb2b_master_reference:16.heading: Key Results to Quote
lakeb2b_master_reference:16.text:
35% shorter sales cycles with custom ICP modelling and territory data
$2.8M+ pipeline generated in documented case studies
Full DPDP, GDPR, and CCPA-compliant infrastructure from day one
Strategic partnership positioning (not a vendor transaction)

lakeb2b_master_reference:17.heading: 3.1 Pricing Model: Credit-Based System
lakeb2b_master_reference:17.text:
LakeB2B operates on a credit-based pricing model. Credits are the universal unit of transaction across all four verticals. Clients purchase credits upfront and redeem them against contacts, enrichment actions, or data exports.
This model gives clients full budget predictability, eliminates surprise invoices, and allows flexibility to apply credits across SalesTech, MarTech, RecruitTech, or GrowthTech as their priorities shift.

lakeb2b_master_reference:18.heading: 3.3 Credit Tier Guidelines
lakeb2b_master_reference:18.text: The following tiers are indicative starting points for sales conversations. They should be adapted based on the vertical, volume commitment, enrichment depth, and deal context. These are not published prices.

lakeb2b_master_reference:19.heading: 3.4 What Credits Cover
lakeb2b_master_reference:19.text: Credits can be applied across all four verticals. Here is how credits are typically redeemed:

lakeb2b_master_reference:20.heading: Standard Contact Record: 1 credit. Includes verified name, business email, job title, company name, phone number.
lakeb2b_master_reference:20.text:
Enriched Contact Record: Additional credits may apply per record for enriched fields such as direct dial, LinkedIn profile URL, intent score, technographic tags, or company firmographics.
Bulk List Exports: Credits consumed based on total record count in the export. Campaign audience builds, segmented lists, and talent searches all draw from the credit balance.
Data Refresh or Re-Verification: Clients who need to re-verify or refresh existing records may have a separate credit rate depending on volume and data age.
Custom Builds (GrowthTech): For bespoke ICP models, territory builds, or compliance-ready infrastructure projects, credits are consumed based on scope. These are typically quoted as project-level engagements.

lakeb2b_master_reference:21.heading: 3.5 Pricing Scenarios: How to Talk About It
lakeb2b_master_reference:21.text: Here are real-world pricing scenarios your team will encounter, along with the recommended approach for each.

lakeb2b_master_reference:22.heading: Scenario A: Small Pilot / Proof of Concept
lakeb2b_master_reference:22.text:
"We just want to test your data before committing."
Response: Perfect. Our Starter package is designed exactly for this. 100 credits, no lock-in, no annual commitment. You test, you validate, and if the bounce rates and reply rates speak for themselves, we scale together.

lakeb2b_master_reference:23.heading: Scenario B: Mid-Market SDR Team
lakeb2b_master_reference:23.text:
"We have 5 SDRs doing outbound. We need about 2,000 contacts a month."
Response: That puts you in the Scale tier, roughly 2,000 credits per month. At the baseline rate, that is $200/month. If you commit to a quarterly or annual volume, we can discuss a volume arrangement that brings your effective rate down.

lakeb2b_master_reference:24.heading: Scenario C: Enterprise Multi-Vertical
lakeb2b_master_reference:24.text:
"We need data for sales and marketing, and we are operating across three regions."
Response: Enterprise engagements start at 10,000+ credits and are custom-quoted. We would scope your usage across verticals and regions, then build a commercial model that works for your projected volume. These tend to be annual agreements with dedicated account support.

lakeb2b_master_reference:25.heading: Scenario D: Competitor Price Comparison
lakeb2b_master_reference:25.text:
"ZoomInfo charges us X. Why should we pay more?"
Response: The right comparison is not list price per record. It is cost per meeting booked. Our bounce rate is under 1% while the industry average sits at 5 to 8%. That means less wasted spend per sequence, higher domain reputation, and more pipeline per dollar. We are happy to run a side-by-side deliverability test on 100 records so you can see the difference for yourself.

lakeb2b_master_reference:26.heading: 3.7 Competitive Pricing Context
lakeb2b_master_reference:26.text: This table is for internal reference only. Never share competitor pricing with clients. Use it to calibrate your positioning.

lakeb2b_master_reference:27.heading: 4.1 Signal-to-Vertical Mapping
lakeb2b_master_reference:27.text: Every outreach should be triggered by a signal. Signals tell you which vertical to lead with. Use the table below during prospect research to match observed triggers to the right LakeB2B solution.

lakeb2b_master_reference:28.heading: 4.2 Pitch Angles by Vertical
lakeb2b_master_reference:28.text: Each vertical has a specific pitch angle. Getting this right determines whether you get a reply or land in the trash folder. The golden rule: lead with outcomes, not features.

lakeb2b_master_reference:29.heading: 4.3 Persona Matrix: Tone by Seniority Tier
lakeb2b_master_reference:29.text: The way you speak to a CEO is fundamentally different from how you speak to a Sales Manager. Use this matrix to calibrate your tone, word count, and CTA style based on the seniority of the person you are contacting.

lakeb2b_master_reference:30.heading: Tone: Friendly, direct, peer-to-peer. Very practical, zero corporate speak.
lakeb2b_master_reference:30.text: What they care about: Making their own quota/targets, tools that save them time, not looking bad in front of their manager.

lakeb2b_master_reference:31.heading: 4.4 Industry Tone Modifiers
lakeb2b_master_reference:31.text: Layer these modifiers on top of the seniority tier. A CTO at a SaaS company needs different language than a CTO at a hospital system.

lakeb2b_master_reference:32.heading: 4.5 Email Framework A: Signal-Based Challenger
lakeb2b_master_reference:32.text:
Use when a specific, verifiable behavioural trigger exists (hiring, funding, tech change, expansion).
Subject Line Format: [Company Name] + [short trigger reference], under 7 words total.
Good: "Acme Corp + APAC expansion" or "Tekflow's Salesforce migration"
Bad: "Quick question", "Following up", "Partnership opportunity"

lakeb2b_master_reference:33.heading: Touch 1 (Day 1, Cold): Signal, Challenge, Proof, Ask. Full structure above.
lakeb2b_master_reference:33.text:
Touch 2 (Day 5 to 7, Follow-Up): Reply to original thread. One-sentence recap of core value prop. Give the easy out ("If this is not a priority for the current quarter, completely understood"). Simple yes/no on current timing.
Touch 3 (Day 12 to 14, Breakup): Brief acknowledgement this is the last note. Leave the door open warmly. One soft final question. "Worth keeping in touch?"

lakeb2b_master_reference:34.heading: 4.6 Email Framework B: Value-First Asynchronous Audit
lakeb2b_master_reference:34.text:
Use when there is no single strong trigger, or when an operational inefficiency signal is detected. This is the best default framework for SaaS, agencies, and data-led outreach.
Subject Line Format: "Idea for [Company Name]'s [specific area]"
Good: "Idea for Acme Corp's data hygiene" or "Idea for Meridian's campaign targeting"

lakeb2b_master_reference:35.heading: 4.7 Post-Reply Protocol
lakeb2b_master_reference:35.text: Once a prospect replies with any degree of interest, the relationship dynamic changes. The sender is now trusted. At this point, and only at this point, you can introduce scheduling mechanics.

lakeb2b_master_reference:36.heading: 4.8 Discovery Questions by Vertical
lakeb2b_master_reference:36.text: Once you are on a call, use these discovery questions to qualify the opportunity and match the prospect to the right vertical. Do not ask all of them. Pick 3 to 5 based on what you already know from research.

lakeb2b_master_reference:37.heading: SalesTech Discovery
lakeb2b_master_reference:37.text:
How large is your outbound team today, and what is your current monthly contact volume?
What is your current bounce rate on outbound email sequences?
Where does your SDR team source their contact data today?
Are you expanding into any new territories or verticals in the next two quarters?
How much time does your team spend on manual list building versus actual outreach?

lakeb2b_master_reference:38.heading: MarTech Discovery
lakeb2b_master_reference:38.text:
What does your current campaign audience build process look like?
What is your cost-per-MQL right now, and where do you want it to be?
Are you running account-based marketing programs? If so, how do you build those audiences?
How often do you refresh or re-verify your marketing database?
What is your biggest bottleneck: reaching the right people or reaching enough of them?

lakeb2b_master_reference:39.heading: RecruitTech Discovery
lakeb2b_master_reference:39.text:
How many open roles is your TA team working right now?
What percentage of your hires come from passive sourcing versus inbound applications?
What tools are you using for candidate sourcing today?
Are there specific skill sets or regions where sourcing is particularly difficult?
What is your average time-to-fill for technical or specialist roles?

lakeb2b_master_reference:40.heading: GrowthTech Discovery
lakeb2b_master_reference:40.text:
What does your current data infrastructure look like, and who manages it?
Are you operating across multiple regions with different compliance requirements?
How do you model your ICP and territory assignments today?
Is your current data provider meeting your accuracy and compliance needs?
Where do you see the biggest data gap holding back your growth plan?

lakeb2b_master_reference:41.heading: 5.1 Data Sourcing and Verification
lakeb2b_master_reference:41.text: LakeB2B maintains a 450M+ contact database through a multi-layered verification process. Understanding this process is critical for answering client questions about data quality and sourcing integrity.

lakeb2b_master_reference:42.heading: How We Source Data
lakeb2b_master_reference:42.text:
Publicly available business registries, corporate filings, and professional directories
Opt-in partnerships and cooperative data exchanges
Web crawling and NLP-based extraction from public company profiles and press releases
Technology install detection (technographic data) via script and DNS analysis
Intent signal partnerships and behavioural data cooperatives

lakeb2b_master_reference:43.heading: How We Verify Data
lakeb2b_master_reference:43.text:
Real-time SMTP-level email verification before delivery (not just syntax checks)
Phone number validation against carrier databases for direct dials
Title and company matching against multiple independent sources
Automated decay detection: contacts flagged for re-verification when job change signals are detected
Human QA layer for enterprise and GrowthTech custom builds

lakeb2b_master_reference:44.heading: 5.2 Compliance Framework
lakeb2b_master_reference:44.text: Compliance is not optional and not an afterthought. Every record in the LakeB2B database is processed under a compliance framework that satisfies the major global privacy regulations.

lakeb2b_master_reference:45.heading: 5.3 Data Freshness and Decay
lakeb2b_master_reference:45.text: B2B data decays. People change jobs, companies restructure, and email domains rotate. This is not a flaw in the product; it is a reality of B2B data. The question is how a provider handles it.

lakeb2b_master_reference:46.heading: 5.4 Client Onboarding Process
lakeb2b_master_reference:46.text:
When a new client signs, here is the standard onboarding flow. Knowing this helps the sales team set accurate expectations during the close process.
Agreement signed and credits provisioned (same day).
Onboarding call: account manager walks client through portal, credit redemption, and data request workflow (Day 1 to 2).
First data request submitted: client defines targeting criteria (ICP, geography, titles, industries).
List built and verified: depending on complexity, delivered within 24 to 72 hours.
Quality check: client reviews sample; adjustments made if needed.
Full delivery and credit deduction.
Ongoing: monthly usage reviews, data refresh cycles, and account optimization.

lakeb2b_master_reference:47.heading: Approved backgrounds: White, light gray, Purple (#6D08BE), Navy (#011A6B)
lakeb2b_master_reference:47.text:
Never do any of the following with the LakeB2B logo:
Alter the colors or fonts of the logo
Skew, rotate, or distort the logo
Add drop shadows, gradients, or effects
Place the logo on busy, patterned, or clashing backgrounds
Merge the logo with other brand marks or subsidiary logos

lakeb2b_master_reference:48.heading: 6.6 Imagery Direction
lakeb2b_master_reference:48.text:
All LakeB2B imagery should communicate the intersection of human expertise and technology intelligence. This includes data visualization graphics, digital platform interfaces, and imagery of modern business environments.
Apply brand color tints (purple, pink, orange neons) and curved line graphics to imagery to align with the LakeB2B visual language. Avoid cold, sterile stock photography. Imagery should feel warm, dynamic, and forward-looking.

lakeb2b_master_reference:49.heading: 6.7 Voice and Tone
lakeb2b_master_reference:49.text: LakeB2B content should be confident, data-driven, and outcome-focused. Avoid jargon for the sake of jargon. Always lead with what the client achieves, not what the product does. Frame LakeB2B as a partner, not a vendor.

lakeb2b_master_reference:50.heading: 7. Frequently Asked Questions
lakeb2b_master_reference:50.text: This section covers the questions your team will hear most often. Internalize these answers so you can respond with confidence and precision.

lakeb2b_master_reference:51.heading: Q: What does LakeB2B actually do?
lakeb2b_master_reference:51.text: A: LakeB2B provides verified B2B contact data and audience intelligence across four specialized verticals (SalesTech, MarTech, RecruitTech, GrowthTech). We help sales, marketing, and recruitment teams reach the right people with accurate, compliant, intent-enriched data.

lakeb2b_master_reference:52.heading: Q: How is LakeB2B different from ZoomInfo, Apollo, or Lusha?
lakeb2b_master_reference:52.text: A: Three things set us apart. First, data quality: our bounce rate is under 1%, while the industry average is 5 to 8%. Second, vertical specialization: we do not sell a one-size-fits-all product. Four verticals mean each buyer gets a solution tuned for their team. Third, compliance: our infrastructure is built for GDPR, CCPA, and DPDP from day one, not bolted on after the fact.

lakeb2b_master_reference:53.heading: Q: Is LakeB2B the same as Ampliz?
lakeb2b_master_reference:53.text: A: No. LakeB2B and Ampliz are both Champions Group companies, but they serve different markets. Ampliz focuses on healthcare data intelligence (physicians, hospitals, clinics) while LakeB2B serves the broader B2B data market across all industries. They share technology infrastructure but operate as distinct brands with distinct go-to-market strategies.

lakeb2b_master_reference:54.heading: Q: How big is the database?
lakeb2b_master_reference:54.text: A: 450M+ verified global executive and professional contact profiles across 7M+ companies. But the number that matters more is the bounce rate: under 1%. A smaller, verified database outperforms a larger, unverified one every time.

lakeb2b_master_reference:55.heading: Q: How much does it cost?
lakeb2b_master_reference:55.text: A: We operate on a credit-based model. One credit equals one contact. The minimum floor rate is $0.10 per contact, which means $1 buys 10 credits. Volume tiers and enterprise packages are negotiated based on projected usage.

lakeb2b_master_reference:56.heading: Q: Is there a free trial?
lakeb2b_master_reference:56.text: A: We offer a Starter package at 100 credits for $10. It functions as a low-risk pilot, allowing prospects to validate data quality before committing to volume. There is no free tier, but the $10 entry point is designed to make the "try before you buy" conversation easy.

lakeb2b_master_reference:57.heading: Q: Do credits expire?
lakeb2b_master_reference:57.text: A: Credits do not expire during the agreement period. Clients can apply them across any vertical at any time. At renewal, if a client has consistently under-utilized their allocation, we work with them to right-size the package.

lakeb2b_master_reference:58.heading: Q: Can a client use credits across multiple verticals?
lakeb2b_master_reference:58.text: A: Yes. Credits are universal across SalesTech, MarTech, RecruitTech, and GrowthTech. A client can use 500 credits for outbound contacts in SalesTech this month and 500 for audience builds in MarTech next month.

lakeb2b_master_reference:59.heading: Q: How do you verify email addresses?
lakeb2b_master_reference:59.text: A: Every email address undergoes real-time SMTP-level verification before delivery. This goes beyond syntax checks. We connect to the receiving mail server and confirm the mailbox exists and accepts messages. This is why our bounce rate stays under 1%.

lakeb2b_master_reference:60.heading: Q: How fresh is the data?
lakeb2b_master_reference:60.text: A: Active accounts are on a 90-day re-verification cycle. We also run continuous job change signal detection. When a contact changes roles, gets promoted, or moves companies, the record is flagged for re-verification. Enterprise clients can request on-demand refreshes at any time.

lakeb2b_master_reference:61.heading: Q: What if the data is bad?
lakeb2b_master_reference:61.text: A: If a client receives contacts that do not meet our quality standards (bounced emails, incorrect titles, wrong companies), we replace them at no additional credit cost. Our sub-1% bounce rate guarantee is not marketing language. It is an operational commitment.

lakeb2b_master_reference:62.heading: Q: Is your data GDPR compliant?
lakeb2b_master_reference:62.text: A: Yes. Our infrastructure is built around GDPR from the ground up. We process B2B contact data under the legitimate interest lawful basis and maintain full documentation. We are happy to share our Data Processing Agreement during procurement.

lakeb2b_master_reference:63.heading: Q: Can I use this data for cold outreach?
lakeb2b_master_reference:63.text: A: Yes, for B2B outreach. Our data is sourced and processed for B2B commercial use. Clients must include proper opt-out mechanisms (as required by CAN-SPAM, CASL, etc.) in their outreach, but the data itself is cleared for B2B prospecting.

lakeb2b_master_reference:64.heading: Q: How do you handle data subject access requests (DSARs)?
lakeb2b_master_reference:64.text: A: We have a documented DSAR process. If a data subject requests access to, correction of, or deletion of their information, we process the request within the regulatory timeframe. Clients can also submit DSAR requests on behalf of their recipients.

lakeb2b_master_reference:65.heading: Q: Can I integrate LakeB2B with my CRM?
lakeb2b_master_reference:65.text: A: Data is delivered in standard formats (CSV, XLSX) compatible with all major CRMs including Salesforce, HubSpot, Zoho, Pipedrive, and Microsoft Dynamics. For enterprise clients, we offer direct API integration and automated delivery pipelines. Discuss specifics with the technical team during onboarding.

lakeb2b_master_reference:66.heading: Q: What fields are included in a standard contact record?
lakeb2b_master_reference:66.text: A: A standard record includes: full name, verified business email, job title, company name, company phone, industry, company size, and location. Enriched records add direct dial, LinkedIn profile URL, intent score, technographic tags, and company firmographics.

lakeb2b_master_reference:67.heading: Q: Can I filter by specific criteria?
lakeb2b_master_reference:67.text: A: Yes. You can filter by geography, industry, company size, revenue range, job title, seniority level, department, technology stack, and intent signals. The more specific your ICP definition, the more precise the output.

lakeb2b_master_reference:68.heading: Elevator Pitch (30 Seconds)
lakeb2b_master_reference:68.text: "LakeB2B is a B2B data intelligence company. We help sales, marketing, and recruitment teams grow faster by giving them access to over 450 million verified contacts, intent signals, and compliance-ready data infrastructure. Everything is priced at ten cents a contact through a simple credit-based model. You buy credits, you get verified contacts, and you build pipeline."

lakeb2b_master_reference:69.heading: Pricing in One Line
lakeb2b_master_reference:69.text: Credit-based model. 1 credit = 1 contact. Minimum floor: $0.10 per contact ($1 = 10 credits). Enterprise pricing is custom.

lakeb2b_master_reference:70.heading: Three Things That Make LakeB2B Different
lakeb2b_master_reference:70.text:
Data quality over data volume: bounce rates under 1% versus industry average of 5 to 8%.
Compliance-ready for GDPR, CCPA, and DPDP from day one.
Partner mindset: we invest in your outcome, not just the list delivery.
